coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 8–10 miles, Z2 (140–150 bpm), rolling terrain. You're in week 25 of a 22-week plan—you're *past* peak and tapering hard. Your last three runs average 12.8 miles at 129 bpm; yesterday's 17.16 was a confidence builder but you're done digging the hole. Tuesday is normally your quality day, but in taper it's a sharpness maintenance run: conversational pace, no intervals, no push. Your RHR is 57 (down from 61.4 avg), recovery score is 100, and you slept 12+ hours—you're fully recovered and your nervous system is primed. Keep it easy so you stay that way.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You're 66 days out and in taper: this is when gut training matters more than volume. On an easy 8–10 miler, run fasted or with just coffee if you habitually do; if you fuel, keep it to 30g carbs (one gel, light bar). Post-run, eat 40–50g carbs + 15–20g protein within 30 min—yogurt + granola, toast + eggs, chocolate milk. Your big travel schedule means consistency beats perfection; pick one portable refuel you can execute in hotel rooms or airports. Alpine 50K aid stations are sparse; practice your race nutrition once weekly on longer efforts so your gut doesn't surprise you Sept 18.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Sleep is your limiting resource right now, not training. You logged 12.12h last night at 96.2% efficiency—exceptional—but your 30-day average is 7.52h. That's taper-phase catch-up working, and it's critical: sleep drives VO₂ max consolidation, glycogen storage, and mental resilience in ultras. Protect 8+ hours nightly through race week. If travel is compressing sleep, use blackout curtains, white noise, and avoid screens 60 min before bed. HRV data is missing (no 30-day baseline), but your low RHR and 100/100 recovery score indicate good parasympathetic tone; keep supporting it with 20–30 min easy movement (walk, yoga) today and ice baths only if you're sore—you're not.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're at the moment where doubt creeps in because volume is dropping and the race feels real. Ignore it. Your fitness is made; these 9 weeks are about arriving fresh and confident, not fit-er. Review your route profile (2,500m vert spread over 50K is ~500m per 10K—steep, sustained), dial in your pace discipline on tomorrow's hill work, and run one dress-rehearsal effort with your full race kit and nutrition plan in the next 14 days. The Julian Alps aren't forgiving, but a sharp, well-rested 40-something beats a tired 30-something. You're tracking perfectly.
